name: AllTech National Settlements
description_short: Real estate title and settlement company providing title insurance, escrow management, and digital closings for residential and commercial transactions in all 50 states.
description_long:
AllTech National Settlements — also operating as AllTech National Title — is a real estate title and settlement services company believed to have been founded around 2008. The company is headquartered in Crafton, Pennsylvania (Pittsburgh area) with a reported corporate presence in Fairfax, Virginia, and provides title and closing services across all 50 states plus Washington, D.C. Despite the word 'settlements' in their name, this refers strictly to real estate transaction settlements — the legal and financial closing process for property transfers — and has no connection to debt settlement, credit repair, or consumer lending of any kind.

AllTech handles the full lifecycle of a real estate closing: title searches to identify ownership defects or liens, title insurance for both residential and commercial transactions, escrow account management, and in-person or fully remote eClosing ceremonies. Their eNotary offering enables remote online notarization, and they provide legal review and specialized construction title and escrow services for developers and builders. A notable operational feature is access to four major underwriters — First American, Stewart, Fidelity, and WFG — allowing the firm to shop competing rates on behalf of clients rather than being locked into a single carrier.

AllTech positions itself around a single point of contact model: one agent manages the entire transaction from title search through final signing rather than routing clients between departments. The company cites 100+ years of combined team experience and employs in-house Senior Underwriters for complex or multi-state commercial accounts. Client satisfaction data is strong — a 5.0/5 Google rating from 74 reviews and a 4.9/5 rating across 41 reviews on Birdeye — though both review pools are relatively small for a national firm.

For real estate professionals, lenders, and buyers or sellers who need a nationally licensed settlement agent, AllTech offers full-service coverage with multi-underwriter access and a digital closing platform. However, there are real limitations to note: no pricing is published anywhere — all fees are transaction-based and require a direct quote, making upfront cost comparison impossible. Their BBB profile was only filed in September 2024 and carries no confirmed letter rating or accreditation. Employee headcount estimates conflict across data sources (10–25 vs. 65+), and at least one complaint involving escrow fund handling has been documented on Birdeye. Consumers seeking credit repair, loans, or debt relief will find no relevant services here.
address: 41 White Ave 2 Crafton Square, Crafton, PA 15205

pricing.guarantee_details: No money-back guarantee policy identified. Contact provider for details.

pros: Access to four major title underwriters — First American, Stewart, Fidelity, and WFG — enables rate comparison rather than single-carrier lock-in
Single point of contact model: one agent manages the full transaction from title search to final signing
5.0/5 Google rating from 74 reviews and 4.9/5 on Birdeye, indicating strong client satisfaction
National coverage across all 50 states plus Washington, D.C.
In-house Senior Underwriters available for complex or multi-state commercial accounts
eClosing platform allows clients to track transaction progress and sign remotely without an in-person closing
Serves both residential and commercial real estate, including specialized construction escrow
cons: No published fee schedules anywhere — all pricing is quote-based, making upfront cost comparison impossible
BBB profile was only filed September 2024 and carries no confirmed letter rating or accreditation
This is not a consumer finance company — offers zero services related to credit, loans, or debt
At least one Birdeye complaint documented involving escrow fund handling
Employee headcount and revenue figures conflict widely across data aggregators, limiting transparency on company scale
best_for: Homebuyers and sellers needing a nationally licensed settlement agent with remote closing capabilities
Mortgage lenders and banks requiring a multi-state settlement services partner
Commercial real estate investors and developers needing construction escrow and title coverage
Real estate attorneys and agents who prefer a single-contact firm that can handle the full closing process
diagnosis: AllTech National Settlements is best suited for real estate professionals, mortgage lenders, and property buyers or sellers who need a full-service, nationally capable title and settlement company to manage the closing process. The multi-underwriter access and eClosing platform are genuine differentiators for clients managing transactions across states. This company has no relevance whatsoever to credit repair, debt relief, or consumer lending — consumers seeking financial assistance or credit services should look elsewhere entirely.
services: eClosing (digital remote closing with progress tracking)
Multi-underwriter rate shopping (First American, Stewart, Fidelity, WFG)
